name: Llerena and Alcazaba de Reina — circular from Berlanga
name_original: Llerena y Alcazaba de Reina — circular desde Berlanga
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Berlanga traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and open countryside, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features a mix of agricultural lands and scattered woodlands, with elevations that provide both challenging climbs and scenic descents. Road cyclists can expect routes that wind through rural areas, occasionally passing through small towns and villages. Best road cycling routes around Berlanga The most popular road cycling route is Llerena and Alcazaba de Reina — circular from Berlanga, a 36.5 miles (58.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 42 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a mix of open roads and views of historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Llerena loop from Valverde de Llerena, a difficult 45.2 miles (72.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains, providing challenging climbs and panoramic views of the surrounding area. Local road cyclists also love the Fuente Obejuna Town Center – Fuente Obejuna loop from Azuaga, a 46.4 miles (74.7 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and local settlements, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Road cycling around Berlanga is defined by rolling hills, open countryside, and rural roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Berlanga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Berlanga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Berlanga?
A: There are over 10 road cycling routes around Berlanga, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region features a mix of moderate and difficult trails.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Berlanga?
A: While Berlanga's routes are generally characterized by rolling hills, there are no routes explicitly classified as 'easy' in the current selection. However, some moderate routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992849368/llerena-and-alcazaba-de-reina-circular-from-berlanga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llerena and Alcazaba de Reina — circular from Berlanga&lt;/a&gt;, offer a good introduction to the region's terrain without extreme climbs.

Q: What kind of challenges can advanced road cyclists expect in Berlanga?
A: Advanced road cyclists will find plenty of challenging routes with significant elevation gains. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Llerena loop from Valverde de Llerena&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.2-mile (72.8 km) path featuring over 1,000 meters of ascent, providing demanding climbs and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Berlanga?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Berlanga are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992849368/llerena-and-alcazaba-de-reina-circular-from-berlanga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llerena and Alcazaba de Reina — circular from Berlanga&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 36.5-mile (58.8 km) loop.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of road cycling routes in Berlanga?
A: Road cycling routes in Berlanga typically range from around 36 miles (58 km) to over 56 miles (91 km). Durations vary based on distance and difficulty, from approximately 2 hours 45 minutes for moderate routes to over 4 hours for more challenging, longer rides.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Berlanga?
A: Many routes pass through rural landscapes and small settlements. You can also find historical sites and viewpoints. For instance, the route through Llerena offers views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5332635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Reina&lt;/a&gt; and passes near the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5332615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llerena&lt;/a&gt; itself, which is a notable settlement.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region boasts several points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of Granada&lt;/a&gt;, or the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman city of Regina Turdulorum&lt;/a&gt;. These can be explored as part of your cycling adventure or as separate visits.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Berlanga?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, which includes rolling hills and open countryside, and the mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Berlanga?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Berlanga, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for riding.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Berlanga?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122453" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna – Church of Ojuelos Altos loop from Azuaga&lt;/a&gt; cover over 56 miles (91 km) and feature substantial elevation changes, providing a full day's challenge.

Q: Are there routes that explore different towns or villages?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31861652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Obejuna Town Center – Fuente Obejuna loop from Azuaga&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads through rural landscapes and local settlements, offering a glimpse into the regional life and architecture.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Berlanga?
A: The terrain around Berlanga is characterized by rolling hills and open countryside. You'll encounter a mix of agricultural lands and scattered woodlands, with routes winding through rural areas and occasionally passing through small towns. Expect both challenging climbs and rewarding descents.
tips: From the road that goes to the center of Reina, you can see the entire Castillo de Reina, which is located on a hill.

The Castillo de Reina, Badajoz is a defensive building and its origins date back to the last third of the 12th century. The remains of what was a Muslim citadel where several towers and a great wall can still be seen are still visible.

The central square of Llerena is the nerve center of the nucleus where the social activity of the municipality is concentrated. The town, beautiful, is also surrounded by a wall that surrounds the town.
